summary: Cathy O'Neil announces that on her 45th birthday, she received approval from her insurance company for bariatric gastric sleeve surgery after six months of working toward this goal. She expects to have the surgery within two weeks. In preparation, she must begin a strict pre-operative diet of protein shakes and celery only, which means giving up coffee temporarily and carbonated beverages permanently. She expresses excitement about proceeding with this major health decision.
key_points: Insurance approval for bariatric surgery came after a 6-month process that began in January
The surgery is scheduled to happen within approximately two weeks
Pre-op requirements include a strict protein shake diet with no coffee or carbonated beverages
This post follows up on her earlier explanation of why she chose to pursue bariatric surgery
money_quotes: Happy birthday to me, folks! I'm 45 today and my present was that I got the call I've been working towards for 6 months.
In preparation I need to start a strict 'pre-op' diet consisting of protein shakes and nothing else except possibly celery.
Wish me luck, friends! I'm super psyched.
key_insight: After six months of navigating insurance requirements, O'Neil shares a personal health milestone, revealing a more vulnerable side outside her usual algorithmic critique.
